zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Bagot於1363年出生在英國斯塔福爾巴格也斯 布羅姆利時，她的父親Sir Ralph Bagot of Blithfield29歲，母親Elizabeth Blithfield33歲。她與John FitzSimon結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1437年在英國英格蘭享年74歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bagot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Baggett   (see also   Baggott  ), from a pet form of   Bagg   2.

variants.name: Baggett
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Norman personal name Bagot, a diminutive of ancient Germanic Bago, from the root bag- ‘to fight’. Compare Bagg 2.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Bagot was born in 1363, in Bagot's Bromley, Staffordshire, England, her father, Sir Ralph Bagot of Blithfield, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Blithfield, was 33. She married John FitzSimon. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1437, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74.

Elle a épousé John FitzSimon. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1437, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Bagot(이)가 1363년 영국 스태퍼드실르 바고트'스 브롬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Ralph Bagot of Blithfield(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Blithfield(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 John FitzSimon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1437년 영국 잉글랜드에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Bagot родилась в 1363 г. в местности багот'с бромли, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Ralph Bagot of Blithfield было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Blithfield, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John FitzSimon. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1437 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет.
